subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.2. DRAWING, SHADING, COLOURING AND MODELLING FOR DESIGN
content_standard: B8 1.2.1. Design: Demonstrate understanding and use of drawing, shading, colouring and modelling media and techniques for creative expression of design ideas.
indicator_description: Explore available manual and digital tools, materials and techniques for drawing, shading and colouring to create designs from lines, simple shapes and forms
examplars:
1. Explore to determine and document the nature and suitability of available manual tools, materials and techniques for drawing, shading, and colouring for appreciation and reflection.
2. Identify, select and experiment the use of available manual tools, materials and techniques for freehand and outline drawing, shading and colouring to create designs from lines, simple shapes and forms for sharing and appraising.
3. Exhibit own freehand and outline drawings and colour work to share design ideas based on lines, shapes and forms for appreciation, appraising and feedback.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.2. DRAWING, SHADING, COLOURING AND MODELLING FOR DESIGN
content_standard: B8 1.2.1. Design: Demonstrate understanding and use of drawing, shading, colouring and modelling media and techniques for creative expression of design ideas.
indicator_description: Demonstrate skills in using available digital tools, materials and techniques for freehand and outline drawing, shading and colouring to create designs from lines, simple shapes and forms
examplars:
1. Explore available digital tools, materials and techniques to determine and document their suitability for drawing, shading and colouring for appreciation and reflection.
2. Identify, select and experiment the use of available digital tools, materials and techniques for freehand and outline drawing, shading and colouring to create designs from lines, simple shapes and forms for sharing and appraising.
3. Exhibit own portfolio of freehand and outline drawings and colour work to share design ideas for appreciation, appraising and feedback.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 1.2. DRAWING, SHADING, COLOURING AND MODELLING FOR DESIGN
content_standard: B8 1.2.1. Design: Demonstrate understanding and use of drawing, shading, colouring and modelling media and techniques for creative expression of design ideas.
indicator_description: Demonstrate ability to generate design ideas and develop models of simple shapes and forms for appreciation and display
examplars:
1. Identify simple products and generate drawings to illustrate templates for creating models of simple shapes and forms using cardboard, Styrofoam, pulp paper and/or clay for reflection, display and appreciation.
2. Develop own drawings of templates and create models of simple shapes and forms using cardboard, Styrofoam, clay and/or pulp paper for appreciation and sharing.
3. Display own drawings, templates and models of simple shapes and forms for appreciation, appraisal and feedback.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of Visual Arts media and techniques and their application in drawing from direct observation/ memory and imagination, print making and weaving
indicator_description: Explore media and techniques in drawing from direct observation/ memory and imagination, print making and weaving to express own views in visual artworks to encourage recording and research skills.
examplars:
1. Identify, describe, classify and record the tools, materials and equipment for drawing from direct observation/memory and imagination, print making and weaving.
2. Test and classify the tools, materials, equipment according to their uses in drawing direct observation/memory and imagination, print making and weaving.
3. Apply the tools, materials and equipment safely to create drawing direct observation/memory and imagination in nature (prints and woven items) for appreciation.
4. Apply knowledge and skills in cleaning-up, storing and maintenance of tools, materials and equipment in a sustainable manner.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of Visual Arts media and techniques and their application in drawing from direct observation/ memory and imagination, print making and weaving
indicator_description: Experiment by using techniques in print making to generate own ideas and make visual artworks
examplars:
1. Identify and use the relevant media and techniques in printmaking to create visual artworks.
2. Apply and design using relevant media and techniques in print making to create own visual artworks.
3. Display printed artworks for appreciation and reflection and use peer- and self-evaluation to review work.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of Visual Arts media and techniques and their application in drawing from direct observation/ memory and imagination, print making and weaving
indicator_description: Apply techniques of drawing direct observation/memory and imagination to make own visual artworks
examplars:
1. Identify and use the techniques in drawing direct observation/memory and imagination to create own artworks.
2. Use various techniques in drawing direct observation/memory and imagination to create own artworks.
3. Display artworks for appreciation and reflection and use peer- and self-evaluation to review works.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate understanding of Visual Arts media and techniques and their application in drawing from direct observation/ memory and imagination, print making and weaving
indicator_description: Experiment with available media and techniques for weaving to create visual artworks
examplars:
1. Identify and use the available media and techniques for weaving to create own woven artefacts.
2. Apply relevant weaving techniques with available media to create own woven artefacts.
3. Display woven artefacts for appreciation and reflection and use peer- and self-evaluation to review works.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply tempo, dynamics and simple forms in music
indicator_description: Tell how fast or slow music is heard and compare and contrast activities and events that are associated with fast or slow music
examplars:
1. Explain Italian terms used in describing the speed of music.
2. Differentiate between fast and slow music in relation to moments on specific occasions.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply tempo, dynamics and simple forms in music
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the benefits associated with soft or loud music
examplars:
1. Explain Italian terms used in describing the dynamics of music.
2. Differentiate between the benefits and harm of loud and soft music.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.1.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate understanding and apply tempo, dynamics and simple forms in music
indicator_description: Internalise and identify aurally basic architectural designs in art, pop and indigenous musical works (e.g., AB, ABA, AA, AABA, ABAB and ABC).
examplars:
1. Listen and tell if the form of an art musical work is binary, ternary or through composed or free fantasia.
2. Listen and tell if the form of a pop musical work is ballad, verse-bridge song, verse-chorus song or verse-chorus-bridge.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B8.2.1.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate understanding of Ghanaian dance forms
indicator_description: Identify various Ghanaian Dance types and their context of performance
examplars:
1. Identify the dance types and categories. (religious, war and sociocultural)
2. Compare and contrast the types and categories.
3. Explore the context of performance.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: B8.2.1.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate understanding of Ghanaian dance forms
indicator_description: Experiment and practise by using the techniques of rhythm (i.e., beat, tempo, intensity), dynamics (i.e. fast, slow, smooth, flow) in dance and drama.
examplars:
1. Demonstrate dance movements with different rhythms and dynamics.
2. Apply relevant media and techniques to choreograph a dance or direct a play..

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to apply the concept of the design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Design and produce your own artworks that reflect a range of different times and cultures
examplars:
1. Research and record relevant information for planning and developing ideas to make visual artworks that reflect any of the following: history, culture, topical issues, natural and man-made environment.
2. Apply recorded ideas, design process, appropriate tools, materials and techniques to create artworks that communicate views about topical issues.
3. Reflect on and explain whether the artwork in progress conveys the intended ideas and meaning for appropriate revision and use peer- and self-evaluation to review work.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to apply the concept of the design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Plan and display own and others' artworks that reflect the history, cultures, physical and social environment
examplars:
1. Demonstrate understanding and skill in defining roles and responsibilities in selecting and preserving a collection of artworks for an exhibition.
2. Collaborate with peers to monitor, assess and report on the exhibition.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to apply the concept of the design process (idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others' artworks that reflect the history, cultures, physical and social environment.
examplars:
1. Analyse and interpret how selected elements, principles and techniques are used in an artwork to express beauty, meaning and culture of a people.
2. Reflect on and make suggestions on how own interpretation and views on artworks of a culture can be improved to give it an identity.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Design and produce own musical genre that reflect the history and cultures, physical and social environment
examplars:
1. Research and select own and indigenous musical genre outside your community that express personal experiences and interests, moods, visual images, concepts, texts, or storylines on example the SSNIT Pension Scheme services, etc.
2. Collaborate to create rehearsal plan for performing the chosen work in B8. 2.2.2.4 Exemplar I, identifying and allocating time to the various aspects singing, drumming, dancing, costume, venue, etc.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Plan and display own and others' musical works that reflect a range of different times and cultures
examplars: 1. Give a class concert (to be video recorded) that begins with a presentation on the style, historical and cultural context of the compositions selected and rehearsed in B8. 2.2.2.4 Exemplar 1.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the ability to use concept of the design process (idea development) to create and display own creative musical art works that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others' musical works that reflect a range of different times and cultures.
examplars:
1. Conduct an enquiry by playing the recorded video clip from B8. 2.2.2.4 Exemplar I to a group of people (including learners, staff and parents) to collect data and write an aesthetic appreciation report.
2. Reflect and refine own work using the feedback from the appraisal and aesthetic appreciation report.
3. Disseminate the findings of your research to the class by giving a presentation.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate how to apply the concept of design process (Idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Design and produce own dance and drama that reflect a range of different times and cultures
examplars:
1. Discuss a dance or drama artist and their artworks.
2. Design and produce own dance piece or play.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate how to apply the concept of design process (Idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Plan and display own and others' dance and drama pieces that reflect a range of different times and cultures.
examplars:
1. Describe how to plan a dance or drama production that reflects the history, cultures, environment and heritage of a people.
2. Choose and prepare a venue to showcase the performance.
3. Rehearse and perform the planned dance piece or drama skit.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
content_standard: Β8.2.2.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate how to apply the concept of design process (Idea development) to produce and display own creative and expressive art-forms that reflect a range of different times and cultures
indicator_description: Organise an appreciation and appraisal of own and others' dance and drama artworks that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
examplars:
1. Invite an audience to watch the performance of own and others' for appreciation and appraisal.
2. Note and record the strengths and weaknesses of the production and performance for discussion and modification of future performances.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: Β8.2.3.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of visual artists that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Identify and discuss visual artists whose work reflected the history, culture, environment and topical issues in the community
examplars:
1. Conduct an enquiry and gather information on visual artists in the community whose work made impact on the history, culture, environment and topical issues.
2. Classify and record information on visual artists in the community and their artworks according to their areas of specialisation/discipline.
3. Select any visual artist in the community and make a presentation on their life, work and influence.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: Β8.2.3.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of visual artists that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Compare and contrast artworks of visual artists that reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues
examplars:
1. Analyse the similarities and differences of themes and techniques used by visual artists in the production of artworks that reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues.
2. Relate artworks to tradition, culture and environmental issues of the community to derive meaning and ideas.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: Β8.2.3.1. Visual Arts: Demonstrate the ability to correlate and generate ideas from creative artworks of visual artists that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Determine the creative design approaches and processes used by visual artists in creating artworks that reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues
examplars:
1. Examine and record the design approaches used by visual artists in the production of artworks that reflect the history culture, environment and topical issues.
2. Analyse, appreciate and appraise the design processes used by visual artists in producing artworks that solved societal issues.
3. Reflect and evaluate the appreciation and appraisal done for refinement.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B8. 2.3.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the capacity to correlate and generate ideas from indigenous creative musical forms and Ghanaian art musicians that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Distinguish different ways musical works of Ghanaian art composers reflect the history, culture, environment and topical issues
examplars:
1. Compare Ephraim Amu and J. H. K. Nketia.
2. Transcribe a popular song that promotes and sensitises the public an emerging topical issue, e.g., the SSNIT Pension Scheme

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: B8. 2.3.2. Performing Arts - Music: Demonstrate the capacity to correlate and generate ideas from indigenous creative musical forms and Ghanaian art musicians that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Appreciate and appraise at least three (3) indigenous and neo-traditional groups within the nation based on their style, instruments, song themes, dance movements, etc
examplars:
1. Research, select and document three (3) indigenous and neo-traditional groups within the nation based on their style, instruments, song themes, dance movements, etc.
2. Compare and contrast two of the selected indigenous types and two of the neo-traditional types.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: Β8.2.3.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the capacity to correlate ideas from creative artworks of dance and drama artistes that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Select and analyse creative artworks of dance and drama artistes, and identify the history, culture, environment and topical issues that are reflected in them for documentation
examplars:
1. Compare and contrast a dance piece and a play of artistes reflecting the history, culture and topical issues.
2. Document and reflect on the key issues in the dance or play that address environment and topical issues.
3. Analyse and generate ideas from dance and drama performances of artistes from other cultures based on the knowledge gained from community experience.

subject: Creative Arts and Design
sub_strand: 2.3. CONNECTIONS IN LOCAL AND GLOBAL CULTURES
content_standard: Β8.2.3.3. Dance and Drama: Demonstrate the capacity to correlate ideas from creative artworks of dance and drama artistes that reflect a range of different times, cultures and topical issues
indicator_description: Organise a group discussion to appreciate dance and/or plays of own and other cultures, environment and topical issues.
examplars: 1. View and analyse a dance and/or drama performance from own or other cultures based on environment and topical issues for documentation.
